name: STEROID CELL TUMOR
definition: LIPID / LIPOID CELL TUMOR characterized by cells resembling typical steroid hormone-secreting cells (lutein cells, Leydig

demographics.Age: wide range of ages; usually 5th-6th decade
demographics.Path: usually < 3 cm yellow nodule with rich vascularity; rarely cystic
demographics.Histo: abundant clear cytoplasm + varying amounts of lipid resembling adrenocortical cells

dominant_finding: areas of low attenuation (= abundant lipid content)
discriminating_features: areas of low attenuation (= abundant lipid content)
NO high-signal intensity on T1WI ← lipid content obscured by fibrous stroma

CT: areas of low attenuation (= abundant lipid content)
MR: NO high-signal intensity on T1WI ← lipid content obscured by fibrous stroma
intense contrast enhancement ← high tumor vascularity
